steps.title: Read the sentence and identify what each blank must do
steps.content:
The sentence reads: This designation is ______ Proto-Nilo-Saharan is only postulated, its characteristics being inferred from the linguistic properties of its 199 daughters. The word however must be set off as a parenthetical adverb, and the clause after it elaborates on why the designation is hypothetical, so a colon is needed to introduce that elaboration after however.
  • Choice A — 'hypothetical, however,': The comma after however turns what follows into a second independent clause joined only by commas, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'hypothetical, however:': The comma correctly sets off however after the first clause; the colon then introduces the elaborating clause that explains the hypothetical nature. ✓
  • Choice C — 'hypothetical. However,': A period ends the sentence before the elaboration begins, severing the logical link between the two ideas and leaving the second sentence oddly disconnected. ✗
  • Choice D — 'hypothetical: however,': Placing the colon before however is incorrect because a colon should introduce what follows, not precede a conjunctive adverb that is set off by a comma. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct pattern
steps.content: The construction hypothetical, however: places the comma after the first clause to set off the conjunctive adverb however, and the colon introduces the elaborating clause. No other choice achieves this two-part punctuation correctly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
